title: Eval Data Leakage Guard Loop
summary: Detects and prevents training, prompt, or documentation leakage into evaluation datasets.
steps: Fingerprint evaluation cases using exact text, normalized text, metadata, and semantic near-duplicate keys.
Scan candidate training, prompt examples, docs, issue trackers, and public examples for overlap.
Quarantine leaked or near-duplicate cases and version the evaluation set.
Add a pre-release leakage check that runs before training or prompt changes are promoted.
Report scores only for clean splits and disclose any known contamination.
stopConditions: too much contamination requires new dataset
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Eval Data Leakage Guard Loop for [evaluation_set, candidate_training_sources, release_process].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Fingerprint evaluation cases using exact text, normalized text, metadata, and semantic near-duplicate keys.
- Scan candidate training, prompt examples, docs, issue trackers, and public examples for overlap.
- Quarantine leaked or near-duplicate cases and version the evaluation set.
- Add a pre-release leakage check that runs before training or prompt changes are promoted.
- Report scores only for clean splits and disclose any known contamination.

Quality gates before completion:
- fingerprints generated
- overlap scan completed
- leaked cases quarantined
- clean split scores reported
- pre-release check added

Stop when one of these is true:
- clean eval version exists
- too much contamination requires new dataset
- owner accepts documented limitation

Finish with:
- fingerprint method
- overlap report
- quarantine manifest
- clean score report

Escalate for human approval before: reusing contaminated benchmarks, publishing benchmark claims.
